     The Numbers Business Report

Think Like a Man (2012)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $91,547,205Details
International Box Office $21,826,559
Worldwide Box Office $113,373,764
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $17,168,068
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $2,696,729
Total $19,864,797
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Based on Steve Harvey's best-selling book, Think Like a Man follows four interconnected and diverse men whose love lives are shaken up after the ladies they are pursuing buy Harvey's book and start taking his advice to heart. When the band of brothers realize they have been betrayed by one of their own, they conspire using the book's insider information to turn the tables and teach the women a lesson of their own.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$33,636,303 (36.7% of total gross)
Legs:2.72 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:80.7% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$12,000,000 (worldwide box office is 9.4 times production budget)
Theater counts:2,015 opening theaters/2,052 max. theaters, 5.9 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $137,550,824

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: April 20th, 2012 (Wide) by Sony Pictures
International Releases: April 20th, 2012 (Wide) (Suriname)
April 20th, 2012 (Wide) (Trinidad)
April 20th, 2012 (Wide) (Curacao)
May 4th, 2012 (Wide) (South Africa)
June 22nd, 2012 (Wide) (Ghana)
Video Release: August 28th, 2012 by Sony Pictures Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG-13 for sexual content, some crude humor, and brief drug use.
(Rating bulletin 2216, 3/28/2012)
Running Time: 122 minutes
Franchise: Think Like a Man
Cast, crew, or production detail: Ensemble
Plot point: African Americans, Relationship Advice, Relationships Gone Wrong, Romance
Subgenre: Romantic Comedy
Source:Based on Factual Book/Article
Genre:Romantic Comedy
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Screen Gems, Rainforest Films
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

Based on Steve Harvey's best-selling book, Think Like a Man follows four interconnected and diverse men whose love lives are shaken up after the ladies they are pursuing buy Harvey's book and start taking his advice to heart. When the band of brothers realize they have been betrayed by one of their own, they conspire using the book's insider information to turn the tables and teach the women a lesson of their own.
